events.name: A Familiar Tune
events.gmPrompt: A traveling bard in the corner strikes up an old, familiar ballad. The hearth is warm, the ale is flowing. For a moment, the road's hardships fade into memory.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. A warm moment. If a player character is a bard, this is a natural RP prompt for them to join in or request a song. No mechanical consequence.
events.description: A bard plays a familiar ballad. The hearth is warm. The road feels far away.

events.name: Local Gossip
events.gmPrompt: The common room is abuzz with hushed whispers. Over a shared plate, the locals gossip about recent strange occurrences in the area.
events.gmGuidance: Plot hook delivery. The GM should use this to drop a local rumour, quest hook, or piece of world-building. If no hook is prepared, the gossip is generic (taxes, weather, a local festival). No mechanical consequence.
events.description: The common room buzzes with local gossip about recent strange occurrences.

events.name: Tense Words
events.gmPrompt: A heated argument breaks out near the bar between rough-looking patrons. It threatens to escalate, but the innkeeper defuses it before a brawl starts.
events.gmGuidance: Colour and tension. The brawl does not happen -- the innkeeper handles it. If a player character intervenes, that is player-driven outside this event. The GM can use the argument's topic as another plot hook. No mechanical consequence.
events.description: A heated argument near the bar. The innkeeper steps in before it escalates.

events.name: Wandering Merchant
events.gmPrompt: A traveling merchant has laid out a small velvet cloth on a corner table, displaying peculiar trinkets, odd charms, and minor curiosities.
events.gmGuidance: Flavour only. The merchant is not a mechanical shop -- this is atmosphere. If the GM wants to make it interactive, that is their call outside the event system. The trinkets are colourful but mundane unless the GM decides otherwise.
events.description: A merchant displays trinkets and curiosities on a velvet cloth in the corner.

events.name: The Local Cat
events.gmPrompt: The local cat has decided. It hops up onto your bed, turns three times, curls into a tight ball against your side, and begins to purr. Loud, steady, and completely unbothered by who you are or what you have done.
events.gmGuidance: Pure comfort. The cat does not care about your backstory. It is warm, you are warm, this is the arrangement. If a player has a familiar or animal companion, the cat either ignores it or washes its face at it. Speak with Animals: the cat wants warmth and stillness. It is not interested in conversation. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: The local cat curls up on your bed and purrs.
